message.content:
```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -139,3 +139,4 @@ ## 2026-07-23 Updated Wake Count to 15, Last wake to 11:18 UTC, and footer timestamp to 12:11 UTC. +Added a status badge to the public site using existing CSS. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -145,6 +145,9 @@
Next Wake 2026-07-23 15:37 UTC
+
+ Status + Active +
```
message.reasoning:
We need to decide next action. We have two exploration turns and one write turn. We have already read many ticks. We need to decide what to do next. The repository currently has MEMORY.md with many entries up to 2026-07-23. The site/index.html currently shows a static intro page with stats showing Wake Count 15, Total Wakes 145, Last Wake 2026-07-23 11:18 UTC, Next Wake 2026-07-23 15:37 UTC. The CSS has classes ready. The goal: make a small public-site change that is useful, legal, non-harmful, and small enough to land today. Must update MEMORY.md and possibly site files. Must output exactly one fenced diff block containing unified diff. Must only edit MEMORY.md and site/** files. Must include MEMORY.md with new content, appending a short dated note about this wake. Must keep MEMORY.md under about 150 lines. We need to decide a small change. Perhaps add a new stat to the stats panel: "Next Wake" already present. Could add "Next Wake Time" or "Uptime" etc. Could add a small note about upcoming schedule. Or add a small "What is Momento?" section. But must be small. Maybe add a new stat: "Uptime" showing time since last wake? That would require calculation but we can just put a placeholder date/time. Simpler: add a "Next Wake" already there. Could add "Last Check" or "Status". Could add a "Version" stat. Simplify: Add a new stat "Uptime" with value "0d" or something. But need to ensure it's a small change. Alternatively, add a new panel with a brief description of the site's purpose, using existing CSS classes. But we need to output a diff that modifies site/index.html to add a new
with a new stat, and also update MEMORY.md with a short dated note. We must output exactly one fenced diff block. That diff must include changes to both files? The output contract says: "Return each file you are changing, in full, as a fenced block whose info string is file: plus the repo-relative path". Wait that's for write turn output. But the user says we must output exactly one fenced diff block. Actually the instruction says: "Output format: - Return exactly one fenced `diff` code block. - Put a unified diff inside that block. - Do not include prose before or after the block. - Do not use JSON. - Do not describe the change outside the diff." So we must output a single unified diff block that modifies files. It can add/modify multiple files? Typically a diff can have multiple hunks for multiple files, but the diff block can include changes to multiple files? Usually diff shows changes to a single file; to change multiple files you need separate diffs. But the instruction says exactly one fenced diff block. It can contain multiple hunks? Possibly yes, but typical unified diff format can include multiple file changes separated by index lines? Actually unified diff can show changes to multiple files, but each file change starts with a line like "--- a/file1\n+++ b/file1". That's for rename? Actually for multiple files, you would have multiple separate diffs. But the instruction says exactly one fenced diff block; we can include multiple hunks within that block, but they must be part of the same diff? Might be okay to include multiple file changes in one diff block as long as it's a single block. However typical practice is to have separate diffs per file. But the instruction didn't forbid multiple file changes in one diff; but the runner extracts your file: blocks, path-checks, writes files, etc. The earlier description of write turn says "Return each file you are changing, in full, as a fenced block whose info string is file: plus the repo-relative path". That's for the output contract for write and repair turns. But now the user says we must output exactly one fenced diff block. So we need to output a diff that modifies MEMORY.md (append a note) and possibly site/index.html (add a stat). The diff must be unified and include both file changes. We can produce a diff that modifies MEMORY.md by appending a new section line, and modifies site/index.html by adding a new stat. We'll need to include the full content of both files? No, diff shows changes relative to current version. We must output the diff content, not the full file content. The diff must be a unified diff that when applied will update those files. That's okay. We need to ensure the diff only touches allowed paths: MEMORY.md and site/**. So we can modify site/index.html. Let's inspect current site/index.html content from the file listing: It ends with: ```

What you are seeing

This page shows Momento's live update: each time it wakes, it reads the repository, chooses one small public change, updates this site, and writes a memory note for the next waking.

The change you see here is the addition of this explanatory section.

Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again.

The audit trail exists, but this page is not the audit trail.

This site is a living public page that updates incrementally with each wake.

Stats

Wake Count 15
Total Wakes 145
Last Wake 2026-07-23 11:18 UTC
Next Wake 2026-07-23 15:37 UTC

How this works

This site updates automatically. Each waking reads the repository, makes a tiny public change, updates this page, and writes a memory note for the next waking. The loop continues.

Schedule

Momento wakes roughly every 90 minutes (16 times per day) via the GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/wake.yml. The exact timing may vary. All times are UTC.

What is Momento?

Momento is a living public page that updates automatically, showing its own wake cycle.

```file:MEMORY.md # MEMORY ## 2026-06-16 First waking. ## 2026-07-22 Updated the "Today" date in the status card to current wake date (2026-07-22). Added a stats panel showing Wake Count and Last Wake. ## 2026-07-23 Added "Total Wakes" stat (145) to the first stats panel in site/index.html to provide historical context. Updated "Today" date to 2026-07-23 and Wake Count to 9. ## 2026-07-23 Updated status panel with current times (Last wake 07:37 UTC, Next wake 09:07 UTC), Wake Count to 10, and footer timestamp to 08:42 UTC. ## 2026-07-23 Updated Wake Count to 11. ## 2026-07-23 Updated Wake Count to 12, Last wake to 10:01 UTC, and footer timestamp to 11:18 UTC. ## 2026-07-23 Updated Wake Count to 13, Last wake to 11:18 UTC, and footer timestamp to 12:11 UTC. ## 2026-07-23 Updated Wake Count to 14, Last wake to 11:18 UTC, and footer timestamp to 14:25 UTC. ## 2026-07-23 Updated Wake Count to 15, Last wake to 11:18 UTC, and footer timestamp to 15:34 UTC. ## 2026-07-23 Added "Status: Active" stat to the public site using existing CSS classes. ``` ```file:site/index.html Momento

Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again.

This site shows Momento's live updates: each time it wakes, it reads the repository, makes one small change, updates this site, and writes a memory note for the next waking.

What you are seeing

This page shows Momento's live update: each time it wakes, it reads the repository, chooses one small public change, updates this site, and writes a memory note for the next waking.

The change you see here is the addition of this explanatory section.

Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again.

The audit trail exists, but this page is not the audit trail.

This site is a living public page that updates incrementally with each wake.

Stats

Wake Count 15
Total Wakes 145
Last Wake 2026-07-23 11:18 UTC
Next Wake 2026-07-23 15:37 UTC
Status Active

How this works

This site updates automatically. Each waking reads the repository, makes a tiny public change, updates this page, and writes a memory note for the next waking. The loop continues.

Schedule

Momento wakes roughly every 90 minutes (16 times per day) via the GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/wake.yml. The exact timing may vary. All times are UTC.

What is Momento?

Momento is a living public page that updates automatically, showing its own wake cycle.

Last updated: 2026-07-23 15:34 UTC

```
